skill.name: mermaid-diagramming
skill.description: Create Mermaid diagrams for Obsidian notes, including flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, Gantt charts, ER diagrams, and architecture diagrams.
skill.summary: Create clear Mermaid diagrams for Obsidian notes and technical documentation.
skill.author: BitYoungjae

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, network, C2, and cryptography patterns, but review shows they are Mermaid documentation syntax and example diagram text. No executable scripts, shell commands, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed files. The only real external references are links to official Mermaid resources, so the skill is low risk and safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Mermaid Syntax Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static command findings match Mermaid code fences, arrows, labels, and example syntax rather than Ruby backticks or shell execution. The cited content is instructional Markdown for Obsidian diagrams and does not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are fenced Mermaid or plain Markdown examples. I found no shell invocation, Ruby backtick execution, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: Security Keywords Are Diagram Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. C2, reconnaissance, credential, and token terms appear inside architecture and authentication diagrams. They describe systems being diagrammed and do not implement network access, credential collection, or persistence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is visual documentation of servers, databases, login flows, and class fields. There is no code path that reads secrets or communicates with an external service.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Keyword Collisions
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Weak cryptography findings align with Mermaid tables and styling syntax, not cryptographic libraries or hashing calls. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, insecure cipher use, or custom encryption logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are arrow, style, and visibility reference tables. They contain no cryptographic API calls or data protection behavior.

low_findings.title: Configuration and Script Patterns Are Documentation Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The script and environment access hits are Mermaid examples for quoted text and YAML frontmatter configuration. They do not read environment variables, import configuration libraries, or execute scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious strings are displayed as diagram syntax examples. I found no process environment access, JavaScript runtime code, or executable configuration loader.

low_findings.title: Benign External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for hardcoded URLs, LOW risk. The skill links to Mermaid official documentation and Mermaid Live Editor as external resources. These links do not transmit user data and are appropriate for a documentation skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links, and the fetch-like text is a class diagram method name. No network request implementation or data exfiltration path was found.

content.user_title: Create Mermaid Diagrams for Obsidian Notes
content.value_statement: Technical notes often need diagrams that stay readable and version friendly. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce Mermaid diagrams that render in Obsidian.
actual_capabilities: Creates Obsidian-ready Mermaid fenced code blocks for common diagram types.
Selects diagram types for workflows, APIs, data models, timelines, and architecture.
Provides syntax guidance for Mermaid flowcharts, sequences, classes, Gantt charts, and ER diagrams.
Explains Mermaid styling, labels, arrows, comments, and theme considerations.
Helps split large diagrams into smaller diagrams for better Obsidian rendering.
Includes references for newer Mermaid beta diagram types supported by Obsidian versions.
limitations: Obsidian may use an older Mermaid version than the public Mermaid documentation.
Very large diagrams can render slowly and should be split into smaller diagrams.
JavaScript callbacks and interactive click actions are not supported in Obsidian for security.
Generated diagrams may need syntax adjustments for a specific Obsidian theme or plugin version.

use_cases.title: Document System Architecture
use_cases.description: Create architecture diagrams that show services, databases, APIs, and deployment boundaries inside Obsidian notes.

use_cases.title: Explain Product Workflows
use_cases.description: Turn requirements, decision paths, and customer journeys into readable flowcharts, timelines, and journey maps.

use_cases.title: Model Data and APIs
use_cases.description: Generate ER diagrams, sequence diagrams, and class diagrams for database schemas, API flows, and domain models.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Flowchart
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian-ready Mermaid flowchart for this process: [describe steps]. Use clear node labels and include decision points.

prompt_templates.title: Diagram an API Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mermaid sequence diagram for this API flow: [describe actors and messages]. Include success and failure paths.

prompt_templates.title: Model a Database Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mermaid ER diagram for these entities and relationships: [list tables, keys, and cardinality]. Keep labels concise.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Full Architecture View
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian Mermaid architecture diagram for this system: [describe services, groups, and dependencies]. Add a short note about rendering limits.

output_examples.input: A user signs in, the app validates credentials, and the API returns either a token or an error.
output_examples.output: A sequence diagram showing the user, client, API, authentication service, and database with success and failure branches.

output_examples.input: A product team needs to compare features by effort and value.
output_examples.output: A quadrant chart that places each feature by relative effort and business value for prioritization review.

output_examples.input: A notes app stores users, workspaces, notes, and tags.
output_examples.output: An ER diagram describing each entity, important keys, and the relationships between users, workspaces, notes, and tags.

best_practices: State the target diagram type and the purpose before requesting the diagram.
Keep node labels short and move detailed explanations outside the diagram.
Test generated Mermaid in Obsidian and simplify diagrams that render slowly.
anti_patterns: Do not pack a whole system into one diagram when separate views are clearer.
Do not rely on JavaScript click callbacks because Obsidian disables them.
Do not use ambiguous labels such as service or process without context.

faq.question: Does this skill create diagrams that work in Obsidian?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on Mermaid fenced code blocks and syntax patterns used by Obsidian.

faq.question: Can it generate flowcharts and sequence diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, Gantt charts, ER diagrams, and other Mermaid types.

faq.question: Does it render diagrams itself?
faq.answer: No. It writes Mermaid diagram text for Obsidian or another Mermaid renderer to display.

faq.question: Can it use Mermaid features from the newest release?
faq.answer: It can suggest them, but Obsidian may not support every new Mermaid feature immediately.

faq.question: Is this useful with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help Claude Code or Codex add documentation diagrams near source files and project notes.

faq.question: How should I handle very large diagrams?
faq.answer: Split them by view, such as context, data model, sequence, and deployment, to keep rendering fast.
